verses.fhb_text: This is the word of our Father to Israel, delivered through Malachi.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Superscription; YHWH resolves to our Father in the narrator's voice.

Renderings:
• "the word of our Father" — YHWH never transcribed; resolves to the Father per FHB-1.2-31, narrator voice.
• "through Malachi" — the prophet's name means "my messenger."
verses.footnote: The name Malachi means "my messenger" — fitting for the book that closes the Old Testament by announcing the messenger who prepares the way.

verses.fhb_text: “I have loved you,” says our Father. “But you ask, ‘How have you loved us?’ Was not Esau Jacob’s brother?” declares our Father. “Yet I have loved Jacob,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The book opens on the Father's wounded love, doubted by his children.

Renderings:
• "says our Father" / "declares our Father" — YHWH speech-formula resolves to the Father (FHB-1.2-31).
• "I have loved you" — first-person divine love opening the disputation.

verses.fhb_text: but Esau I have rejected. I turned his hill country into a wasteland and left his inheritance to the jackals of the wilderness.”
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The covenant-election contrast (Jacob loved, Esau set aside).

Hebrew/Greek: sane'ti (I hated) → "I have rejected" — covenant-election idiom of preference, not personal malice.

verses.fhb_text: Edom may say, “We have been shattered, but we will rebuild the ruins.” But this is what the Father of heaven’s armies says: “They may build, but I will tear down. They will be called the Wicked Land, the people with whom our Father is angry forever.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Judgment on Edom shows the Father's protective justice in history.

Renderings:
• "the Father of heaven’s armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot, no Adonai present (FHB-4.4.1-2).
• "our Father is angry forever" — third-person YHWH reference resolves to the Father.

verses.fhb_text: You will see it with your own eyes and say, “Great is our Father—even beyond the borders of Israel!”
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Israel's anticipated confession of the Father's greatness over all nations.

Renderings:
• "Great is our Father" — yigdal YHWH; covenant-insider confession resolves to the Father (FHB-4.1-1).

verses.fhb_text: “A son honors his father, and a servant his master. If then I am a father, where is the honor due me? And if I am a master, where is the reverence due me? says the Father of heaven’s armies to you priests who despise my name. But you ask, ‘How have we despised your name?’
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father names himself Father and asks for the honor his children withhold.

Renderings:
• "If then I am a father" — the Father's explicit self-identification (FHB-1.2-27, Mal 1:6 anchor); first-person, no padding.
• "the Father of heaven’s armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot, no Adonai (FHB-4.4.1-2).
verses.footnote: Here our Father names himself Father outright and asks his children for the honor a son naturally gives — the heart-cry that runs through the whole book.

verses.fhb_text: “You place defiled food on my altar. But you ask, ‘How have we defiled you?’ By saying that our Father’s table may be despised.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Defiled offerings expose contempt for the Father's table.

Renderings:
• "our Father’s table" — shulchan YHWH; third-person reference resolves to the Father (FHB-1.2-31).

verses.fhb_text: When you bring blind animals for sacrifice, is that not wrong? When you sacrifice lame or diseased animals, is that not wrong? Try offering them to your governor! Would he be pleased with you? Would he accept you?” says the Father of heaven’s armies.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Blemished sacrifices would shame even a human governor.

Renderings:
• "the Father of heaven’s armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot, no Adonai (FHB-4.4.1-2).

verses.fhb_text: “Now plead with our Father to be gracious to us. With such offerings from your hands, will he accept any of you?” says the Father of heaven’s armies.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: A pointed call to seek the Father's grace while offering him refuse.

Renderings:
• "plead with our Father" — chalu-na p'ne El; the prophet's address to the people, divine name resolves to the Father.
• "the Father of heaven’s armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot (FHB-4.4.1-2).

verses.fhb_text: “Oh, that one of you would shut the temple doors, so that you would not light useless fires on my altar! I take no pleasure in you,” says the Father of heaven’s armies, “and I will accept no offering from your hands.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father would rather the temple close than receive empty worship.

Renderings:
• "says the Father of heaven’s armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot (FHB-4.4.1-2); surrounding speech first-person.

verses.fhb_text: “My name will be great among the nations, from the rising of the sun to its setting. In every place incense and pure offerings will be brought to my name, because my name will be great among the nations,” says the Father of heaven’s armies.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father's name worshiped worldwide — the missional vision against Israel's failed worship.

Renderings:
• "my name will be great among the nations" — Father's first-person declaration.
• "the Father of heaven’s armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot (FHB-4.4.1-2).

verses.fhb_text: “But you profane it when you say the Sovereign Father’s table is defiled and its food may be despised.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The priests profane the Father's table by treating it with contempt.

Renderings:
• "the Sovereign’s table" — shulchan Adonai; Adonai renders "Sovereign" (FHB-4.4.1-2), never "Lord" (reserved for Jesus).

verses.fhb_text: “And you say, ‘What a burden!’ and you sniff at it in contempt,” says the Father of heaven’s armies. “When you bring stolen, lame, or diseased animals and offer them as sacrifices, should I accept them from your hands?” says our Father.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Worship offered as a wearisome chore grieves the Father.

Renderings:
• "the Father of heaven’s armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot (FHB-4.4.1-2).
• "says our Father" — bare YHWH speech-formula resolves to the Father (FHB-1.2-31).

verses.fhb_text: “Cursed is the cheat who has a sound male in his flock and vows to give it, but then sacrifices a blemished animal to the Sovereign Father. For I am a great King,” says the Father of heaven’s armies, “and my name is to be feared among the nations.”
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father is the great King whose name the nations will revere.

Renderings:
• "sacrifices... to the Sovereign" — Adonai → "Sovereign" (FHB-4.4.1-2).
• "I am a great King" — Father's first-person self-description.
• "the Father of heaven’s armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot (FHB-4.4.1-2).

chapter_notes: Malachi 1 is governed by FHB-1.2-27 (Malachi anchors): the book is the OT's closing Father-heart text, climaxing in 1:6 where our Father names himself Father and asks for the honor his children withhold. YHWH is never transcribed (FHB-1.2-31), resolving to 'our Father' in narration and first-person divine speech. YHWH-Tzva'ot renders 'the Father of heaven's armies' (FHB-4.4.1-2, no Adonai present); Adonai (vv.12, 14) renders 'the Sovereign.'
